methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $150M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Heirloom Carbon, a Series B-stage climate and energy company founded by Shashank Samala, is evaluated against a comparable universe of 9 peers (1 public, 8 private) in the same sector. The private peer set includes Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc., spanning valuations from $120M to $5.0B.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the company's $150M+ total raised as a proxy for Series B entry value and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for the next funding stage, implies a valuation of $285M. Peer-median analysis across the six same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $355M, with the peer range spanning $120M to $5.0B.

Triangulation of these two methods produces a central estimate of $320M, with a low bound of $242M and a high bound of $408M. The peer-median approach carries material weight given the deep comparable set, though the wide dispersion within that set ($120M–$5.0B) reflects substantial heterogeneity in technology maturity and market expectations within early-stage climate tech. Key uncertainties include technology readiness, path to commercialization economics, and capital intensity of scale-up—factors not captured in stage-based or peer-median mechanics alone.
